required_next_action: Keep the July edition internal until June closes, then finalize the provisional classifications for the 22 known June public updates and write the public 2026-07 edition from reviewed records.
blockers: June 2026 has not ended yet; this cannot be a final retrospective edition before 2026-07-01.

The June 15 trial-watch report has 22 stale or missing registry checks and 0 open stale watchlist records; do not make fresh trial-status claims without surveillance or explicit stale-record boundaries.

open_checklist_items.label: Review field-activity watchlist counts, public activity lens summary, and unresolved capture candidates before drafting field-change copy.
Wait until the June 2026 retrospective period closes before publishing data/content/state-of-the-field/2026-07.json.
Write the public July edition from reviewed records after the period closes.
Run validation, state-of-field status, editorial ratchet, typecheck, and build before publication.
open_checklist_items.command: npm run validate:records && npm run state-of-field:status -- --strict && npm run audit:editorial:ratchet && npm run typecheck && npm run build

decision_sections.title: Include as field signals
decision_sections.guidance: Use for reviewed period events that changed the visible field read without resolving the overall LEV proof gap.

items.event_name: Published Senomorphics Field-Change Update for June 7, 2026
items.event_summary: Staged update adding the June 2026 mianserin/calcium-homeostasis mouse lifespan source, refreshing two registry-linked human study records, and keeping the public stage unchanged.
items.event_change_note: The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_biomarker_signal, momentum steady, and confidence low. The new mianserin source adds animal lifespan and human-cell senescence support but does not replace the need for treated-human trials.
items.rationale: June field-change update adding mianserin/Ca2+ animal lifespan and human-cell senescence support while preserving the track stage.
items.next_action: Consider as a June field_signal, bounded as preclinical and not treated-human proof.

items.event_name: Published Autophagy Induction Surveillance for June 11, 2026
items.event_summary: Field-change update adding a 2026 older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot and refreshing the Autophagy Induction outlook while keeping the track at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal with mixed momentum.
items.event_change_note: Add the older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot as the strongest new human signal, but preserve human_biomarker_signal, mixed momentum, and low read firmness because the result is small, subgroup-limited, and not broad healthy-aging benefit.
items.rationale: June field-change update adding a small older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot while preserving the early human biomarker stage.
items.next_action: Consider as a June field_signal, bounded as small, subgroup-limited, and not proof of lasting aging-relevant function.

items.event_name: Published Microbiome Composition Modulation Field Change Check for June 3, 2026
items.event_summary: Field-change update for the microbiome-composition-modulation track, adding a new disease-specific synbiotic constipation RCT and a null healthy-older-adult exercise/microbiome RCT while keeping the public stage at a tentative human biomarker signal.
items.event_change_note: Keep the stage at a tentative human biomarker signal, change momentum to mixed, and add support-map evidence for the new TBC169 RCT and null older-adult exercise/microbiome RCT.
items.rationale: June field-change update added a disease-specific synbiotic constipation RCT and a null healthy-older-adult exercise/microbiome RCT while keeping the track at a tentative human biomarker stage.
items.next_action: Consider as a bounded June field_signal or as current_context for microbiome evidence boundaries.

items.event_name: Published Live Biotherapeutics and Engineered Microbes Field Change Check for June 6, 2026
items.event_summary: Field-change update adding a new Pediococcus acidilactici PA53 older-adult well-being and gut-health RCT while keeping the track at a tentative evidence that human function improves with no LEV-relevant timing upgrade.
items.event_change_note: Add PA53 as a second bounded older-adult human signal while keeping the evidence stage at evidence that human function improves, momentum accelerating, the read tentative, and no LEV timing upgrade.
items.rationale: June field-change update added a Pediococcus acidilactici PA53 older-adult well-being and gut-health RCT while keeping the track tentative and without a LEV timing upgrade.
items.next_action: Consider as a bounded June field_signal if the final edition needs a microbiome/live-biotherapeutic example.

decision_sections.title: Include as current context

items.event_name: Published Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control missing-context update for June 6, 2026
items.event_summary: missing-context bundle that stages missing reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor biomarker and trial records to watch, TPN-101/ARPA-H activity, DREAM/somatic-mutation biology, TET2 CHIP canakinumab trials to watch context, and recent TE review triangulation while keeping the track at a tentative human biomarker signal.
items.event_change_note: Repair improves missing source context and public boundary wording for RTI biomarkers, trials to watch activity, TPN-101 program momentum, DREAM biology, and CHIP trials to watch context, but does not change stage, confidence, momentum, or LEV-relevant timing because controlled human intended-target and functional evidence remain absent.
items.rationale: June missing-context update that narrowed reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor, trial, program, DREAM, and CHIP boundaries without changing stage, momentum, read firmness, or LEV timing.
items.next_action: Classify as June current-context unless the final edition needs it as a named example of better claim boundaries.

items.event_name: Published NAD and Redox Restoration Missing-Context Update for June 8, 2026
items.event_summary: Missing-context update that adds MIB-626 human NAD augmentation records, NR/NMN/MIB-626/MitoQ no-results trials to watch, and recent review context while keeping the track at a tentative human functional stage.
items.event_change_note: The update improves source completeness and public watchlist boundaries, but does not change the track stage, momentum, or read firmness because the new MIB-626 and registry records remain short, no-results, disease-context, review-level, or not tied to long-lasting outcomes.
items.rationale: June missing-context update that improved MIB-626, registry, and review boundaries without changing stage, momentum, or read firmness.
items.next_action: Classify as current_context unless a trial-horizon item needs explicit NAD/redox coverage.

items.event_name: Published Mitophagy Enhancers Missing-Context Update for June 10, 2026
items.event_summary: Missing-context update that adds urolithin A safety and limiting-context sources, stages the most relevant active or completed no-results urolithin A registries, refreshes registry metadata for published trials, and updates the Mitophagy Enhancers outlook evidence map while keeping the track at tentative human_biomarker_signal.
items.event_change_note: Keep human_biomarker_signal, steady momentum, and a tentative read. The staged repair improves safety, registry, limiting-context, and evidence-map coverage but adds no long-lasting improvement in older-human function.
items.rationale: June missing-context update that added urolithin A safety, registry, and limiting context while preserving the track read.
items.next_action: Classify as current_context, with possible trial_horizon support after stale registry boundaries are checked.

items.event_name: Published Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support update for June 10, 2026
items.event_summary: Added arimoclomol NPC open-label extension and current Miplyffa label safety context while keeping Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support at low-confidence, NPC-specific human_functional_benefit with mixed momentum.
items.event_change_note: Repair NPC durability and safety context, add current label boundaries, and preserve human_functional_benefit, mixed momentum, and low read firmness because the evidence remains NPC-specific.
items.rationale: June context update added NPC open-label extension and current Miplyffa label safety context while preserving a disease-specific, mixed read.
items.next_action: Classify as current_context if the July edition needs a proteostasis example.

items.event_name: Published Senolytics Coverage Repair for June 11, 2026
items.event_summary: Coverage-repair bundle staging Senolytics source, study, finding, intervention, registry-watch, review, animal-anchor, and outlook-support records. The proposed outlook refresh keeps the track at moderate-confidence human_biomarker_signal with mixed momentum because human biomarker support is now auditable, while functional benefit remains preliminary, mixed, disease-specific, or no-results.
items.event_change_note: Keep Senolytics at human_biomarker_signal with mixed momentum and moderate confidence. The support map is now adequate, but functional benefit remains preliminary and controlled human evidence includes null or limiting results.
items.rationale: June missing-context update added D+Q and fisetin biomarker, function, safety, animal, and no-results registry context while keeping senolytics at an early human biomarker stage.
items.next_action: Classify as current_context unless senolytics is chosen as a flagship boundary example.

items.event_name: Published Partial Reprogramming Coverage Repair for June 12, 2026
items.event_summary: Coverage-repair bundle staging Partial Reprogramming source, study, finding, registry-watch, review, safety-boundary, seminal-anchor, and outlook-support records. The proposed outlook refresh keeps the track at low-confidence animal_signal with accelerating momentum, updates the human boundary to no human outcomes evidence, and adds ER-100 as a no-results phase 1 trial-watch item.
items.event_change_note: Keep evidence_stage at low-confidence animal_signal and momentum accelerating. Update the human boundary from no direct human intervention evidence to no human outcomes evidence, with ER-100 recorded as a recruiting phase 1 no-results registry.
items.rationale: June missing-context update strengthened the animal source map and added ER-100 as a no-results human registry boundary while preserving animal_signal and accelerating momentum.
items.next_action: Classify as current_context or a frontier boundary item; keep the absence of human outcomes explicit.

items.event_name: Published DNA Repair Enhancement Surveillance for June 10, 2026
items.event_summary: Field-change update attaching the existing June 2026 Nat Aging DREAM repression evidence to DNA Repair Enhancement and refreshing the track outlook while keeping the track at tentative human_biomarker_signal with mixed momentum.
items.event_change_note: Add a broader DREAM repair-regulator signal while keeping the track at human_biomarker_signal, mixed momentum, and a tentative read because the new result is preclinical/comparative rather than treated-human evidence.
items.rationale: June field-change update attached DREAM repression evidence to DNA repair enhancement and refreshed the track while keeping it at a tentative human biomarker stage.
items.next_action: Classify as a mechanistic field_signal or current_context; avoid implying tested systemic human repair enhancement.

items.event_name: Published Immune Rejuvenation Coverage Repair for June 11, 2026
items.event_summary: Coverage-repair bundle staging mTOR/TORC1 older-adult immune-function anchors, an RTB101 phase 3 limiting record, a direct immune-aging recruiting registry, a recent review/taxonomy-boundary record, and a revised support-mapped Immune Rejuvenation outlook. The proposed outlook keeps the track at human_functional_benefit but changes momentum to mixed and confidence to low.
items.event_change_note: Keep the human_functional_benefit stage because human immune-function endpoints exist, but downgrade confidence to low and momentum to mixed because support is branch-specific, RTB101 did not replicate in phase 3, active registries have no posted results, and durability/safety remain unresolved.
items.rationale: June missing-context update added older-adult immune-function anchors, RTB101 limiting context, direct immune-aging registry context, and review boundaries while keeping the track at human functional benefit but lowering the read to mixed and tentative.
items.next_action: Consider as a June outlook-boundary item; be explicit that the clearer read is not an overall LEV upgrade.

items.event_name: Published Rapalogs Coverage Repair for June 11, 2026
items.event_summary: Coverage-repair bundle staging Rapalogs source, study, finding, registry-watch, review, seminal-anchor, and outlook-support records. The proposed outlook refresh moves the track to low-confidence human_functional_benefit with mixed momentum because human functional endpoints have been tested, but the evidence remains branch-specific, mixed, and short of durable disease, frailty, mortality, or broad healthy-aging benefit.
items.event_change_note: Replace the activity-only moderate human_biomarker_signal outlook with a source-backed low-confidence human_functional_benefit read. The stage changes because human functional endpoints have been tested; confidence lowers because PEARL is mixed, RTB101 has a phase 3 miss, and many registries remain no-results.
items.rationale: June missing-context update moved rapalogs to a tentative human functional read with mixed momentum because human functional endpoints have been tested, while durable disease, frailty, mortality, and broad healthy-aging benefit remain unproven.
items.next_action: Decide whether rapalogs should be the prominent June outlook-boundary item in what_changed.

items.event_summary: Staged surveillance update adding the June 2026 Frontiers in Aging APRC-CM multimodal healthy-aging pilot, refreshing stale registry metadata for four published EV/secretome human study records, and keeping the public stage, momentum, and confidence unchanged.
items.event_change_note: Keep evidence_stage human_biomarker_signal, momentum accelerating, and confidence low. The APRC-CM pilot adds a human biological-age biomarker signal but does not isolate conditioned media from lifestyle and supplement components or establish functional benefit.
items.rationale: June reviewed field context: the APRC-CM pilot adds a small human biological-age biomarker signal and registry-hygiene updates, while preserving the EV/secretome track's stage, momentum, read firmness, and no-functional-benefit boundary.
items.next_action: Carry this as bounded June current context in the July edition if space permits; do not treat it as a field-moving result unless later controlled functional evidence changes the outlook.

decision_sections.title: Post-hoc material correction

draft_seed.title: June 2026 in review
draft_seed.bottom_line: LEV did not look closer after the reviewed June updates. A stronger read would still require repeated human studies showing long-lasting improvements in health or function.
open_questions: After June 30, decide which June public updates belong in what_changed versus current_context.
Decide whether rapalogs should be a prominent June item because the public read moved from activity-only human biomarker context to a source-backed tentative human functional read.
Re-check any stale registry claims before turning trial horizon seed items into public trial_horizon copy.
Run the narrative rollup after publishing the July edition if it becomes the current story's observed State of the Field edition.
field_activity_guidance: Use current field movement only as activity or context unless reviewed evidence changes an outlook.
Treat NewLimit Series C, Retro financing, and ARPA-H PROSPR as activity or trial-horizon context, not evidence of benefit.
Treat Calico, Altos, NewLimit formation, XPRIZE, Hevolution, and Calico collaboration records as historical field context, not June progress.
Keep activity-only and assessment-changing counts separate so the July edition does not imply that funding, launches, or prize infrastructure changed LEV timing.

summary_lines: Retirement candidates needing final sweep or explicit decision: 4
Active/recruiting/planned records past local completion date: 11
Completed or past-completion records with unresolved no posted results: 25
Active result-watch records with missing or stale registry checks: 0
surveillance_use_lines: Re-check every NCT in the active result watch before a scoped surveillance no-op.
Use late no-results records as low-priority watch context, not as public anticipation that results are still expected.
Do not mark a trial `retired_no_results` until the retirement sweep is recorded in a research session.
Treat unchanged no-result registries as surveillance facts, not field progress.
Treat status-only changes as activity unless they alter the public interpretation.
Send posted trial results through staged evidence review before changing an outlook.

agent_recommendations: Keep the edition internal until the publication gate passes; do not move it to in_review or published while gate issues remain.
Use the recorded agent decisions as the starting classification set; no raw reconciliation decisions are currently waiting for curator classification.
Do not ask for approval unless new evidence, surveillance, or field-activity candidates appear; the current approval packet is empty.
Keep source-work field-activity candidates out of public monthly copy unless a durable source is found or a curator approves an exception.
Review the State-of-Field-routed field activity lens before drafting; treat activity as context or trial horizon unless reviewed evidence changes an outlook.
Rerun the trial audit after the covered period closes and refresh any registry-status claims before final trial_horizon copy.
Use the draft seed as scaffolding only; final public JSON should be written from reviewed records after period close.
